title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 19 - The Enemies! - Part 2
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_UWA2fCxRHI

Daniel (@dungeondive) continues the enemy survey with the Flesh Stalkers, the Scafford Gang, the Thunder Warriors, and a rapid-fire tour of the smaller enemy packs. The Flesh Stalkers are the standout—ancient alien scientists who perform sinister experiments on their mindless Flesh Drone minions, splicing DNA from other enemy types to create unpredictable hybrids mid-mission. Colonel Scafford's mutant outlaw gang brings ranged firefights, hideous mutations, and named lieutenants.

The Thunder Warriors—clearly inspired by the Three Storms from Big Trouble in Little China—are Forbidden Fortress fan favourites with elemental storm augments (rain, wind, lightning, thunder) and a movement-penalising storm aura. Daniel then speed-runs through every remaining small enemy pack he owns: Serpentman Riflemen, Tongue Demons, Temple Dogs, Flesh Mites, Scourge Rats, Hell Vermin, Swamp Slugs, Dark Stone Brutes, Swamp Raptors, Harvesters, Custodians of Targa, Troon Hunters, Dark Stone Hydra, Scorpions, Bone Eaters, and Magma Fiends. He flags the controversial Ancient Horrors pack—three base-set tentacles on a bigger base—as the moment he started questioning his spending.

----

Flesh Stalker experiments or Thunder Warrior storms—which enemy mechanic would you most want in your campaign?
